Lecture Transcription

Contemplating on sense objects leads to falls down

dhyāyato viṣayān puṁsaḥ
saṅgas teṣūpajāyate
saṅgāt saṣjāyate kāmaḥ
kāmāt krodho ’bhijāyate

[Bg 2.62]

[While contemplating the objects of the senses, a person develops attachment for them, and from such attachment lust develops, and from lust anger arises.]

When contemplating the objects of the senses, one develops attachment. In such attachment lust awakens and when lust is unfulfilled, anger arises. So, anger is born and frustrated material desires. Is that true? For you?

So therefore, Krishna says in the very beginning one must curb this deadly sinful enemy known as lust. We must conquer the lower self with the higher self. Through the process of devotional service, we practice keeping our senses constantly engaged in positive devotional activities. The idle mind is the devil's workshop. When allow idleness to come into our mind, then our senses become very disturbed and restless and then naturally anger will come because you can never completely satisfy the senses. So first of all, we have to create an atmosphere within our life, which is preventative, which means we must keep our mind and senses always engaged in devotional service. And Bhaktivinod Thakur has said that if you can control the tongue, you can control all the other senses. So we must practice very diligently to control our tongue. What does that mean?

Avoid gossip or criticising devotees

First of all, we should not engage in gossip talk which is not constructive to the spiritual progress of others. The tendency of the uncontrolled tongue is to speak of so many worldly affairs or speak critically of devotees of the Lord. When we speak critically of the devotees of the Lord, not only does it pollute our own mind and heart with material contamination, but it pollutes the entire atmosphere. So therefore, the first thing we should do is no gossip, no rumors, no criticism. So what are we going to do with our tongue? Because this is the natural tendency of everyone in Kaliyuga is gossip, rumors and criticism. Huh? We should dovetail the propensity of the tongue to glorify the devotees, to glorify the Lord and to chant the holy names.

[Devotees:] “Hare Kṛṣṇa Hare Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Hare Hare Hare Rāma Hare Rāma Rāma Rāma Hare Hare"

We should not allow our tongue to go anywhere away from the glorification of Krishna at any time. We should only speak what is required for our devotional service.

Be careful about what you eat

And second of all, if you over eat too many opulent foods, it creates disturbance in the belly, disturbance to one's sexual propensities and disturbance to the mind. And then you are very, very prone toward anger. You become like fire. Your whole (your whole) consciousness becomes like raging fire. So we should only eat simple prasad, what is required and we should only speak the positive glories of the Lord and his devotees, chanting his holy names. And this is the process by which we can defeat this deadly enemy of anger, lust. If we do not practice this carefully, from material contemplation comes lust. When we become angry we lose our intelligence and gradually we progressively fall down from the spiritual path. And when momentarily you find you are inflicted by anger, you have to immediately cry out for Krishna to save you. And if you catch that you have already made a mistake, then you should immediately beg humbly the forgiveness of whoever you have offended in your anger. Gradually you will learn not to be angry anymore.

Are there any other questions?

[Devotee:] [05:58-06:13 inaudible]

[Maharaj:] Krishna is protecting you. Krishna is protecting you. I might come in October. Yes?

[Devotee:] How can I increase my strength and determination for devotional service? Seems like it’s just I am becoming aaa.. I am becoming very slack so how should I especially when I have a baby?[Laughter]

[Maharaj:] Why don’t we ask your baby. [Laughter]

[Devotee:] So what do you do, if you have a baby, you can’t become Krishna conscious?

You have to see the baby not as yours. You have to see the baby as the property of God. He is in your care, and in this way we are attached to the baby’s soul.

[Devotee:] No later she becomes older and begins to understand what we have to chant then...

[Maharaj:] No No. Then the baby starts taking care of itself. Right now the baby is completely at your mercy. Your Krishna consciousness will influence its entire life to come. You see to the needs of the body, mind and soul of the child, as Krishna wants you to care for the child at every step. And every bit of affection, every bit of care you offer your child is directly devotional service. The illusion is to think that your children are separate from your service. for Grihasthas children are your service but it's not your only service. But if you think it's something separate, then you're irresponsible in last hope. Krishna may trust you with making jewellery, worshipping the deities. He has also trusted you with children. It is your responsibility and nobody else’s. If that child goes back to God. If you spoil it, you're saying you're misusing the property of God. And if you neglect it, you're sitting and misusing the property of God. You have to see your child as a responsibility that Krishna has given you. He’s a major part of your service to the Lord. So your determination comes from hearing and chanting about Krishna. And also determination comes from hearing and chanting about worldly affairs. As simple as that. Its a very simple formula; if you're always hearing and chanting the glories of the Lord and always singing everything in relationship to the service of the Lord, then your determination will constantly be growing. But if you're hearing rumors, gossip or ordinary mundane affairs and you're speaking those things, then you lose all your determination. In other words, by remembering Krishna, our determination for Krishna consciousness grows. By forgetting Krishna, our determination for Krishna consciousness goes.That means we have to perform the austerity of minimizing our sense gratification and keeping our ears and our tongue constantly engaged in Krishna’s service.

[Devotee:] We hear theoretically it’s right you know.. you should consider your baby to be spirit soul, Krishna’s property but its like really hard to think like that because it’s so easy to be my baby, my child, care for all its bodily needs, so what are the things that one should do so you can be constantly meditating ?

[Maharaj:] The same thing, you have to hear. You’re hearing the Bhagavatam, you're hearing stories like that of Chitraketu Maharaja. It awakens your intelligence to withstand the truth. Essentially everything comes down to what we're hearing and what we’re talking. If you're hearing from the sadhus and you're hearing from the shastras, you will naturally have fresh understanding of the position of your child and the position you're in as a mother in the service of God. But if you're not hearing, then you will forget and most simply think in terms of I and mine. There are nine processes in devotional service. They all are based; they are all situated in the foundation of hearing and chanting. Without properly hearing and chanting you cannot afford any of the other processes of the devotional service properly. And if you're properly hearing and chanting, naturally you’ll exhibit the qualities, which are recommended for all of the nine processes of devotional service. So the question is, are you sincere? Then with great love, enthusiasm to perform the austerity of hearing and chanting.

[Devotee:] Do you mean hearing and chanting means what you chant the 16 rounds and the classes one listens at the temple or the tapes you listen to, is that what you mean by hearing? And association too?

[Maharaj:] Yes. Hearing also means inquiring submissively as you're doing now right now. Right now you could be sitting on the bench talking about so-and-so but instead you're here asking about how you can become more serious about Krishna conscious. Right? You are speaking, you are chanting and you’re hearing. Depending on what the subject matter is, will depend on what your consciousness becomes.

[Devotee:] This one problem I have; I have spoken like this before- it goes on for sometime, it goes on nicely but again it comes back and it’s the same thing and so it is constantly bothering me.

[Maharaj:] What does Bhagavatam say?

naṣṭa-prāyeṣv abhadreṣu
nityaṁ bhāgavata-sevayā
bhagavaty uttama-śloke
bhaktir bhavati naiṣṭhikī

[SB 1.2.18]

 

[By regular attendance in classes on the Bhāgavatam and by rendering of service to the pure devotee, all that is troublesome to the heart is almost completely destroyed, and loving service unto the Personality of Godhead, who is praised with transcendental songs, is established as an irrevocable fact.]

By regularly, daily hearing the Bhagvatam from the great souls and by loving service to them, all the inauspicious things of our heart are destroyed. The tendency is to forget. Therefore we must be constantly endeavouring to inquire, to hear the message of Vasudeva. That is the test of your sincerity.

[Devotee:] [15:00-15:13 indistinct]

[Maharaj:] You can bring a horse to water but you cannot make him drink. Your responsibility is the same, whether they accept it or not. Your responsibility is to be a perfect example of a devotee and to always show them compassion and concern and care for their soul. Therefore if a child no longer wants to be a part of the spiritual path, it is your responsibility to provide a stable foundation in his life that he can always turn to by remaining very strictly fixed on the path yourself. (Your child always) children become rebellious at one stage. Then the rebellious stage passes, right. All the hippies of the sixties are now businessmen living in middle class suburbs. Where are the rebellious people now, what is that, except a few people at the rainbow gathering [laughter]and they have mostly found some spiritual path So it's natural for many children to become rebellious at a certain age but they'll always be in the back of their minds thinking of their parents especially if the parents show them compassion. And the parents are always welcoming them back on the path of Bhakti. If you’re strict and you’re fixed, eventually they will come back and follow you. It’s just a matter of time. But if you deviate, then they have very little hope of ever coming back. They have nothing to come back to. You give in to their maya, you’re slaughtering them. You're putting them in a hopeless condition. If you’ve given them Krishna consciousness, whatever advancement they’ve made is there. They're going to come back; it's just a matter of time. You shouldn't feel bad. It's just a cycle that they have to go through till they can continue on. There should be no regrets, unless we've grossly mistreated them.

[Devotee:] Some parents, they may feel some duality living here in the community of devotees [18:07 - 18:09 inaudible] for surrender and then the kids are getting big and they want to experience maya, so it’s like seems to me that no one knows what to do with those kids, get them out of New York or keep them here or basically no one knows what to do.

[Maharaj:] I think this will have to be evaluated for each individual child. Can’t make a general statement. What’s best for each individual child, has to be decided by parents and mutual devotees.

[Devotee:] There’s a story in Christian tradition of St Augustine; he was very very sinful for many years; for about thirty years - from his teenage years until he was middle aged but his mother prayed for him everyday for thirty years and eventually he became a great saint, probably the greatest Catholic saint, theologian. The church recognises both of them as great saints. St Monica and St Augustine. I was wondering, these two new people, Aerial and Aurbindo recommending that maybe the community should fast one day a week just for the sake of the guilty.

[Maharaj:] You’re asking someone from India; that the community has to decide.

[Devotee:] No, I’m just wondering if you've heard in the scripture or philosophy a similar idea to fast for the sake of someone else’s purification

[Maharaj:] The whole concept of preaching is sacrifice for someone else’s purification. Exactly how that sacrifice is done must be decided by the parents and spiritual master. The greatest sacrifice is not just fasting. That may or may not help but the greatest sacrifice is to give up all your sense gratification twenty-four hours a day every day to set a perfect example for your child. That's the sacrifice of a preacher. Every parent should be an acharya for his child or children or her children. That is the real sacrifice. If you're not doing that, then whether you fast or not, it's not going to have too much of an effect. It may. Fasting is good but it's not enough. Sometimes we fast and we think oh, I’m exempt from any other austerities for the rest of the week. Right? I've done my quota. The real sacrifice is living the philosophy.

[Devotee:] [21:40-22: 10 indistinct]

[Maharaj:] But they were also perfect living examples of pure devotees of the Lord. Someone said from his very infancy, his childhood, his father was a pure devotee so with or without asking the sadhus for the blessings, Prabhupada was perfectly situated. That was just one of his, that was just his mood of pure devotion, but he was a surrendered soul. He wasn't substituting surrender for some ritual. Rituals are there, rituals are good but they have to be built on the foundation of surrender. Not that they are a substitute; that is the tendency.

[Devotee:] [22:53-23:10 indistinct]

[Maharaj:] You have to do what I do, not what I say. Children are very impressionable. What a child needs more than anything is a sense of security in spiritual life. Spiritual life includes material life too. Your child senses insecurity; that will create a very bad consciousness that will perhaps last for the rest of his life or her life. When there's divorce or separation, when there’s fighting amongst parents; that creates a very painful insecurity in the child's life, even if it's a baby, even if it's an infant. It's recognizing what's happening subconsciously and for the rest of that child's life, it may be very very difficult for it to ever get over that insecurity that has been invested within you and it's own parents. If the child perceives an insecurity in the parent spirituality, that will also have a tremendous effect on the child's life. He may never overcome that. So what does it mean to be a parent? It means to sacrifice. It means whether you like your husband or wife or whether you don't, it’s your duty to your child to get along, to stay together. If you don't, that means you have no love for your child. It's all sentimental nonsense. If you're not willing to chant your rounds, (if you're not willing to) your child sees you better than anyone else. Even though he may not intellectually understand what you’re doing. If the child sees that you're not strictly following the principles of Krishna consciousness, that's going to create a tremendous spiritual instability in the child. You're robbing the child, you're depriving it of the opportunity that it deserves. This is what we mean by sacrifice; that you put your husband or your wife and you put your children and you put your (…) first and you put yourself last. If you're not willing to do that, if you're living for yourself for your own feelings, for however difficult [26:00-26:04 indistinct] That's called sacrifice and that sacrifice is Krishna consciousness and that's the expression of love. That's the main problem. It's easy to just blame everybody else but this is the main problem. We have to understand very scrutinizingly and surely what it means to be a responsible parent.

[Devotee:] What if there’s violence in the family? Then how can you owe it to your children in case[26:46-27:48 inaudible]

[Maharaj:] What do you do after the fact?

[Devotee:] No, I mean do you owe it to your children to stay in that marriage or not as far as. Are you obligated to that partner?

[Maharaj:] Depends on the particular circumstances. You have to balance out which is going to be more traumatic (for the parent) for the child. Separation is a tremendously traumatic experience for a child. Violence is also. The question is can the husband and wife come together in Krishna consciousness enough to create the proper atmosphere where they're willing to put aside these quarrels and live for the sake of the children. For the sake of God, living for the sake of children means for the sake of God because they’re God's children. Now if it's absolutely completely out of hand then you should consult personally an advanced saintly person and what is the best course of action to take. This is a case perhaps of the lesser of two evils but you should know that neither one is good.

[Devotee:] [28:17-29:11 indistinct]

[Maharaj:] The Bhagvatam, seventh canto; we’re about to approach this verse in our morning classes, describes the duties of the wife and how yes, what you are saying is true. The wife should be very dutifully bound to be subordinate to the husband if the husband is not fallen. If the husband is not fallen, a wife has no responsibility to follow his fallen condition. Absolutely no responsibility. We find in the case of Gandhari who is considered the most chaste of all women; her husband was blind and she was a beautiful young princess. The most beautiful woman in the world. She had probably 20/20 vision. But when she found out that she was to marry a blind man, she made a vow to cover her eyes and never see the light of day for the rest of her life. Why? Because she felt that if she could see and her husband couldn't, she would feel herself superior to her husband and she would not be able to be his servant. So she submitted to him in that sense but when Dhritarashtra performed abominable activities, she never stood behind him for those. She was faithful, but she never shared his sinful activities with him. She was always giving him good instruction.

Yes?

[Devotee:] You just mentioned that she only followed him into the forest because she was chaste and she was faithful before even though she didn't submit to it. So that means that a wife can be faithful to her husband, but she doesn't get the sinful reaction if she does not submit into what the husband is doing, if it’s wrong. So she won't get the reaction if she's not submitting? That's what you mean?

[Maharaj:] Yes

[Devotee:] I do not understand that whatever it is, if the husband does something wrong it means you got to suffer but if you don't submit then you don’t get the reaction.

[Maharaj:] This is a secret for all you Matajis. Of course all you Matajis who have to be sanyasis don’t have to worry about these things.

[Devotee:] [32:00- 32:25 indistinct]

[Maharaj:] That is your vision but you are not a chaste Vedic woman so you do not understand the fine points of this matter. She understood that pride is the worst enemy of an aspiring devotee. In the Vedic system the husband is a representative of the guru. Just like the disciple is never supposed to present himself to know anything better than the guru. So she did not want to feel superior to her husband. Her joy in life was not to see; her joy in life was to serve. And for her service attitude to be perfect, she accepted this principle.

[Devotee:] Simply it could be another form of pride.

[Maharaj:] It could be, but it wasn't for her because she did it out of genuine devotion. Any activity could be potentially a form of pride if it’s Krishna's in your heart though.

Yes?

[Devotee:] [33:45-33:55 indistinct]

[Maharaj:] Yes, of course. But nobody asked about that aspect yet. Lord Ramachandra was the perfect husband and the duty of the husband is to protect the wife. When Sita was abducted, Ramachandra was willing to fight an entire war to protect his wife. A husband should be willing to fight an entire war with the most deadly Rakshasas on earth to protect his wife. That's his service.

[Devotee:] [34:40-34:55 indistinct]

[Maharaj:] To sacrifice his life, to sacrifice his body, mind, words and life, to properly direct his family on the path to Krishna consciousness. To create all facilities for the wife and children, to be properly situated in devotion to God. That's a great (that's a great) service. Everyone is a servant. No one is a master. The wife is completely immersed in serving the husband by being faithful and chaste. The husband is completely immersed in serving the wife and the children by leading the proper example and by properly providing for them and protecting them. And that's his service, right? Protection means providing what's necessary spiritually and materially. It's not that it's easier to be a husband or a wife. It's not easier to be a wife or a husband. The hard thing is to become Krishna conscious in any circumstance.

[Devotee:] [36:14-36:28 indistinct]

[Maharaj:] What is the role of everyone: the servant of Krishna. Now not only according to the body, but according to the propensity that you are given, according to your previous karma. You have to act according to the laws of God. If a woman wants to imitate a man, she will be exploited by men. The unnatural condition of the society of women's liberation is simply, Prabhupada said, it was actually planned by the man to create an arrangement where the women can easily be exploited by them. Right? Women's liberation movement, they are thinking that oh just see how much progress we're making, but the fact is it's a demoniac plot by the men to create a kind of women that can just be totally exploited for their sense gratification and their sinful desires. If a man and a woman have sex together, free sex liberation, what happens? The woman gets pregnant, the man's gone. He's free. He could do anything he wants. He's liberated, right? But what about the woman? She has two choices; either to commit murder where she's going to have to take birth in hundreds and hundreds of wombs and never see the light of day (that's the sinful reaction for abortion), or she’s going to have this child. And when she has a child, can she work and make a living? No. A new mother can't go out and get a job, so who’s going to care for it? She's on her own, she's liberated. So what is she going to do? She has a child so the best that she can do is she goes through welfare and lives off the state and gets the taxpayers money. And usually she doesn't even care for the child much and the child becomes just a miscreant on the street; a gangster causing tremendous disturbance on the street because he's never been properly cared for or loved. So is that liberation? The man's free, he's gone. So there's a difference and we can't just be completely impractical and say there isn't a difference. It's not that one is superior to the other, it's that (there is) women have to be protected. And when women don't feel they have to be protected, they're exploited. A man, a pious saintly man they preach that women should be protected. And the sinful exploitative man who are ruling this world now, they preach that women shouldn't be protected, they’re equal. They should be given the direction. Then they can go out and have sex anytime and with anyone they want; free sex. And let the woman pay the consequences.

[Devotee:] Its mostly protection from self-gratification.

[Maharaj:] Maya means sense gratification. When I was hitchhiking around the world I remember, in Italy. I was hitchhiking with one friend of mine. We were just fearlessly going anywhere. We didn't even know where we were going. We would just get on the highway and put our thumb out. Somebody would say pick us up and say where are you going and we would say where are you going? Still kind of like that. We never had a destination (we were just), we believed in God. We didn't know who he was, but we believed that he was directing us through the driver, whoever he happened to be. So we just we would be on our way to one place and we'd end up-we’d be on our way to Switzerland and we'd be on a Greek island the next day, you know. So we never had any problems! But I remember one time in Italy, these two American girls; they ran up to us with tears in their eyes. They were hitchhiking too together. They said could you go with him and you go with you, let's change. Why do you want to do like that? Because (every part) every time anyone picks us up, they try to have sex with us. Every single time. You know there’s two of us. These truck drivers pick us up and they start taking off their clothes and this and that. I said I never have that problem. These days you know, Kaliyuga has progressed; there's women truck drivers. That’s why I don’t hitchhike anymore. But you know, it's a fact. Men could defend themselves. Women don't exploit men like that. Men are stronger than women physically; therefore women are very vulnerable to be exploited. How many times do you read about women raping men, huh? Violent rape of a woman to a man? Never. You never see it. Women have a subtle way of raping men. But as far as gross, never happens. So therefore physically, there is a difference. And therefore, women should be put in a situation where their sanctity can be preserved. And then also, it's naturally going to be a different kind of arrangement. It's very practical.

Yes?

[Devotee:] So are men and women equal in the spiritual sense but not in the material sense?

[Maharaj:] In the spiritual sense every living being is equal. Spirit soul is beyond the bodily concept. It is as long as you're living in the world, there's so many bodily conceptions that people are receiving life through and we have to protect ourselves so that our spiritual sanctity can be preserved. In the physical level certain ways women are superior, certainly ways men are superior. Besides this baby. Shamadasi has a baby. Now when the baby wants milk and Rishikesh is holding it, he’s inferior. He's useless. He's totally useless. But she's superior because she can give the child what it wants. But if someone comes to attack the child, Rishikesh is superior. Well, maybe not. (Laughs).

[44:00-44:05 indistinct]

Yes. We’re not for men or for women, we are for the purity of everyone. We are for the purification and sanctity of everybody. You see, the propaganda is to put people in a position where they can preserve their sanctity. They consider that as chauvinism or something like that. But no. It's actually love. The saintly persons teach the doctrine given by God out of love, out of true respect. I remember one time they were asking Srila Prabhupada why women don't have complete freedom to do anything they want. Of course men don’t either. Prabhupada was explaining that to protect a woman is a sign of loving a woman, actually only in a spiritual society is the real respect for the integrity of a woman. In material life there isn't any true respect for the integrity of a woman because they're seeing the woman as an object of sense gratification. Those in spiritual circles are seeing a woman as a spirit soul in a particular body that deserves every right to go back to Godhead. That's real respect. A saintly person sees every woman except his wife as a mother; that is the highest respect. In material consciousness, you see every woman except your mother as your wife, or potentially. Of course that's the extreme but that extreme is very common today.

[Devotee:] In the verses you were reading, the one you read this morning, there is so much restriction on male and female associating especially for [46:32-47:37 indistinct] man or women is married, should he or she also not associate freely with other married men or women?

[Maharaj:] Not for sense gratification.

[Devotee:] [46:51-47:16 indistinct]

[Maharaj:] Preach and chant Hare Krsna. Duty of the sanyasi by his example or her example, by example and by words to give guidance to the rest of the society. And it's the duty of the society to humble themselves and accept the able guidance of those of the renounced order, provided that guidance is based on scripture and example. Without such spiritual [48:12-48:13 indistinct] within our society, we are living examples of the truth, there's practically no possibility of the Grihasta ashram progressing properly. It's like in India recently, there was a feeling amongst certain devotees that people do not take seriously renounced monks in robes; sadhus. What they want to see is men of action; that the best preachers should be the grihastas and they should wear suits and ties and they should preach like that too. They should be professional persons. And (renunciation should be) renounced order should be discouraged. So we were explaining that the varnashram systems yes, the majority of the population will be grihastas. We have professions, occupations and we're preaching and we’re living a perfect example. But unless there's the renounced order of life that they people in the renounced order that they can look up to, they are constantly reminding them and checking them. the grihasta ashram turns into[49:34-49:36 indistinct]. Without the influence of sanyasis, it's impossible to be a grihasta. We become a [49:44-49:46 indistinct]; practically impossible. If you read in the scripture all of the great new grihastas, they all humble themselves to those in the renounced order. Can you think of one example otherwise? Try to think of one. Bhaktivinod Thakur was the perfect grihasta. But by his own example, he was always humbling himself before those in the renounced order; Gaur Kishore Das Babaji, Jagannath Das Babaji, Maharaja Yudhishthira, Dasharatha Maharaj, a great powerful king, a perfect grihasta, a perfect devotee. When Vishwamitra Muni came to his house, simply because of his renunciation, simply because he was a sanyasi, he got off his throne and worshiped the King and offered his services to him with great humility. So this varnashram system is essential; that there has to be a renounced class of men who are giving a very high standard to influence the grihastas. And then yes, they can be in a position (not) very powerful in the service of the Lord. One time they asked Prabhupada, if we take over the world can we make you the King? Prabhupada said no.
